description: When I first observed this bird it was facing toward me and I noted based on it's long wings, slightly crested head without an eyering and behavior that it was a Pewee, however instantly felt that it was atypical for the expected Western Wood-pewee, as it showed almost no vesting to the front and was very pale on the entire chest. Upon closer examination I noted the bird also had an entirely orange lower mandible without the typical duskiness of WWPE. At that time the bird started flycatching and returning to the same general area so I was able to see the back and wings. The bird showed two clear and bright wing bars, nearly white in color, which was much brighter than I had seen in WWPE. The upper wing bar was nearly as bright as the lower one. I watched and photographed the bird for a bit, hoping it would vocalize on it's own, however to that point it did not so I played a recording of Eastern Wood Pewee. As a response the bird vocalized twice a nice clear PEEEE-A-WEEE that matched the recording for Eastern Wood-Pewee I had played.
photographer: Photographer: Cathy Sheeter, Cathy Sheeter, Cathy Sheeter, Cathy Sheeter
